INTRODUCTION
International trade is the lifeblood of the global economy, and for over a century, the Letter of Credit (L/C) has served as its foundational instrument for risk mitigation. By substituting a bank's creditworthiness for that of an importer, the L/C establishes essential trust between cross-border parties. However, despite its critical role, the underlying mechanics of the traditional L/C remain tethered to archaic, paper-intensive processes. Governed by strict compliance doctrines, the modern trade finance ecosystem is heavily burdened by disparate data silos, manual verification

Market Structure Analysis and Production:
Abstract
Purpose - This study aims to integrate industrial organization theory with microeconomic production analysis to investigate the determinants of firm performance in Vietnam's seafood processing industry. The primary objective is twofold: to quantify market concentration and to empirically estimate the industry's production function.
Design/methodology - The research utilizes an unbalanced panel dataset of 435 seafood processing enterprises from 2012 to 2016, sourced from the General Statistics Office and the Vietnam Chamber of Commerce and Industry. An augmented Cobb-Douglas production
THE IMPACT OF ECONOMIC GROWTH ON ECONOMI:
ABSTRACT
This study examines the impact of economic growth on economic complexity across nine ASEAN member states over a 20-year period from 2004 to 2023. Employing a panel data regression model with Robust Standard Errors to address heteroskedasticity, the research investigates both macroeconomic and structural determinants of the Economic Complexity Index (ECI). Contrary to initial hypotheses, the empirical results reveal that short-term economic expansion (GDP growth) and trade openness exhibit a negative impact on economic complexity.
